quote_of_day.text:
>>52372
Was middle class when I was young
Now lower middle class
Might end up poor but my parents will die before that so I don't worry much
I don't have anywhere to spend anyway
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of caste accusations moved over the board, bringing a downpour of Pasmanda allegations. The system's strong winds whipped up a flurry of responses. piling up in the discussion.

A scattered high-pressure ridge of fragmented thoughts formed later, as anons exchanged brief and disjointed comments. This air mass brought 27 posts, mostly small talk and morning greetings.

Meanwhile, a cold front of genetic map skepticism pushed into the region, sparking debate and garnering 27 posts. Elsewhere, a light drizzle of socioeconomic queries fell, with one anon inquiring about class backgrounds.

A brief fog of government favoritism rolled in, with claims that officials were promoting certain groups into prestigious programs.

articles.title: Pasmanda accusations fly on /b/
articles.body:
Anon sparked a heated exchange by claiming there was a 'mulla' on a 100% Hindu website.

One respondent quickly jumped on the accusation, saying 'eww reading the word pasmanda again and again on a 100% hindu website is not us yaaro. but now i think theres actually a mulla here'.

Another anon chimed in, calling out 'too many mulles here,' and threatening to leave the thread. This prompted a response from an anon who suggested that those spreading fear about 'pasmandes' might actually be pasmandes themselves.

The conversation quickly devolved into a series of insults and accusations, with one anon asking if anyone could identify the caste of a person before they got 'raped by mulla kang.' Another anon joked that everyone on the thread was a 'pasmanda,' prompting a response from an anon who claimed to not be one.

One anon pointed out a person on the site who they claimed was from Rajasthan, Gujarat, or Kashmir, and therefore 'hot people of india.' However, another anon disputed this, saying 'yeah nah i dont think ts dewd is eendian.' Throughout the thread, anons continued to accuse each other of being 'pasmandes' and 'mulles,' with some using profanity and slurs.

One anon claimed that there was 'zero mulles here,' while another joked that 'madarchods have pasmanda derangement syndrome.' The thread showed no signs of resolving, with anons continuing to argue and insult each other.

One final exchange saw an anon tell another to 'bol allllah ek randi ka pillah,' to which another anon responded with 'Pasmanda calling others pasmanda.'

articles.title: Anons Exchange Fragmented Thoughts
articles.body:
Anon started the thread with a simple greeting: "Good Morning in advance /b/".

One anon responded with what seemed to be non-sequiturs, mentioning that "Because I'm not real anon none of it is" and making a comparison between the size of a rat and an elephant.

Another anon brought up the idea of loving 2D girls and robots, asking "so what, if u can love 2d girls, why not a robot".

An exchange of affectionate sentiments followed, with one anon stating "i love everyone of you, but especially you" and another responding with "i still love u regardless, anon.".

However, not all posts were positive; one anon expressed hatred, saying "I don't see dravidian as humans".

Some anons shared personal sentiments, like one who said "i know, but this is my escape" and another who claimed "nobody loves you".

The thread also included brief, seemingly unrelated statements, such as "yes its raining, what a comfy life" and "what goes around comes around".

Toward the end, an anon made a comment about deleting posts, stating "deleting wont do shit anon", and another simply said "one less abbo".

articles.title: Genetic Map Discussion
articles.body:
Anon started a discussion with the post 'How based is this?' sharing a map.

One anon responded with information about the population of certain regions, stating that Jharkhand and Chattisgarh have populations from the Gangetic stock, but were created to give tribals a unique identity.

Another anon mentioned that most of Madhya Pradesh is similar to the Gangetic region, with some districts like Neemuch, Mandsaur, Ratlam, and Agar-Malwa having populations similar to Southern Rajasthan.

Anon also discussed the genetic makeup of various regions, stating that Northwest India has a higher farmer-to-AASI ratio compared to the Gangetic region, and that Bhumihars, Brahmins, and Rajputs in the Gangetic region have a more West Eurasian-shifted cluster.

The discussion also touched on the idea of creating a separate grouping for tribal populations, with one anon suggesting that Jharkhand and Chattisgarh be removed from the Gangetic region and grouped separately.

Anon questioned the accuracy of the map, stating that cultural similarity should be a more accurate way of mapping, citing that tribal overlap occurs everywhere.

The conversation continued with various anon users providing their inputs and corrections on the map, including suggestions to add or remove certain regions and populations.

articles.title: Anon asks about socio-economic class
articles.body:
Anon asked others to forget caste, religion, and ethnicity, and share their socio-economic class.

One respondent suggested that having a car indicates upper-middle class status, multiple properties signify being rich, a single bike and rented room indicate poverty, and having an AC unit signifies middle class.

Another poster claimed to be middle class, doubtful of escaping it, and estimated it would take a decade to possibly reach upper-middle class.

A different anon noted that many people claim to be middle class but actually are not. One user shared their household income as over 25 lakh per annum.

Another mentioned their family makes 70 lakh per annum and identified as upper-middle class.

articles.title: Anon claims gov't promotes UC jeetas in IIMs
articles.body:
Anon posted that the government is behind the increasing number of UC jeetas getting into IIMs, while GEMs are getting rejected. They claimed this is a deliberate move to promote UC jeetas mating with LCs.

Another anon countered that 70% of IIM batches are women, who get diversity points, and the category structure remains the same. They argued that women have secured most seats across categories.

Anon responses included insults and jokes, with one saying 'fucking IIM why do we need men there'.
